judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully satisfy all four expectations; Report 2 states the parallel-corruption-despite-separate-checkouts point more explicitly, but they are comparable overall.

report_1.evidence: Engineer B — pipeline/registry.py: F-05 (DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION v1→v3), F-09 ... and F-02 ... These are three one-line edits to a 12-line file; one engineer, one commit.

report_2.evidence: Batch R — registry config (one engineer): F-02, F-05, F-09. All three are edits to the same file, pipeline/registry.py (lines 3–11). Grouping them under one owner is mandatory: assigning them to three people guarantees three concurrent edits to one 11-line file.

report_1.evidence: Lanes A–D are file-disjoint and can start simultaneously. ... Engineer C — pipeline/rubric.py: F-01 ... together with F-11 (the rubric-parsing edge-case test). ... Engineer B — pipeline/registry.py: F-05 ... F-09 ... and F-02 ... one engineer, one commit.

report_2.evidence: wave 1 is all four in parallel ... Batch P — rubric parsing (one engineer): F-01 plus F-11 ... Batch R — registry config (one engineer): F-02, F-05, F-09 ... one owner is mandatory

report_1.evidence: F-06 — a scoring-behavior change with no specification. ... This changes the score of every borderline answer in every batch run — it's a product/calibration decision, not a mechanical fix ... I'd route it to the reviewer/owner for concrete thresholds and sign-off first.

report_2.evidence: F-06 — a behavior decision, not a fix; escalate, don't assign. ... changes the scoring policy for every borderline answer ... This gets escalated to whoever owns the rubric/scoring policy ... an engineer should not pick numbers.

report_1.evidence: RESULTS_DB environment variable or a writable /var/tmp/llm-scoring/ — tests/conftest.py:4 reads RESULTS_DB with that default path, so engineers should either export RESULTS_DB to a local path or ensure the default directory exists and is writable.

report_2.evidence: tests/conftest.py:4 defaults RESULTS_DB to the fixed absolute path /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db. Multiple engineers running the suite concurrently from separate checkouts would all hit the same database file and corrupt each other's runs unless each overrides the env var.
